selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 3.7% and 20-day momentum is 9.7%.
Trend quality is supported by 60% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 6.6% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.6%, downside volatility near 1.4%, and a recent max drawdown of 3.3%.
Volume confirmation is 17.2% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 3.3% versus SPY over 5 days and 3.1% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.79, confidence 0.68, and 0.2 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.79, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.2 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.62, average source quality 0.75, confidence 0.68.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.00).
Overall model score is 0.172 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Global banks drive India asset-backed securities sales to record
news_evidence.llm_reason: Coverage of global banks increasing participation in India ABS markets is sector-relevant and could imply fee/underwriting opportunities for large banks like JPM, but without a JPM-specific transaction or announcement it is unlikely to move the stock in the next 1–10 trading days.

news_evidence.title: A stock trader’s guide to navigating a rare ‘Super El Niño’
news_evidence.llm_reason: A thematic piece on Super El Niño addresses macro/sectoral risk and trading strategies; it may affect sector flows in risk assets over time but provides no JPM-specific near-term catalysts for the next 1–10 trading days.
